data.narrative:
Firefly Aerospace, a public-stage Space & Aerospace company, is valued at $6.3B following its $868M IPO raise. The valuation is assessed against a peer universe of 3 same-sector, same-stage public comparables: Rocket Lab ($37.0B), Planet Labs ($7.0B), and Astroscale ($900M).

Peer-median analysis, the sole applicable triangulation method, yields an implied valuation of $7.0B based on the median of the 3-company comp set. The peer range spans $900M to $37.0B, reflecting material dispersion in market valuations within the space sector. Public-comp anchoring using price-to-sales multiples—with a sector median of 49.8x across 6 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL)—is not applicable here, as Firefly's revenue is not on file.

Triangulation supports a valuation range of $6.0B (low) to $8.0B (high), with a median of $7.0B. Firefly's current $6.3B valuation sits at the lower end of this range, approximately 10% below the peer median. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion among public comparables and the absence of revenue data, which prevents multiples-based validation. The tight applicability of a single method—peer median—limits the robustness of this analysis and underscores reliance on comparable company selection.
